decisions.summary: Extract the registration core out of projectAddCommand into a new server-safe module src/lib/project-registration.ts exporting registerProjectFromPath({path,name?}) -> {key,config}. Both the CLI (projectAddCommand) and the new POST /api/projects route consume it. No chalk/console/execSync in the helper.

Registration core may keep the existing SYNC write door (registerProjectSync) and sync hook symlinks (bounded one-shot, sanctioned by NFR-2). ONLY the `git init` subprocess must be async (execAsync/spawn) per NFR-1/PAN-70 — PAN-70 targets unbounded subprocess blocking, which only git init is here.

'new' mode ordering for no-partial-write (AC-3): derive key -> dup-check (reject before ANY fs change) -> compute target <parentDir>/<slug(name)> -> reject if target exists & non-empty -> mkdir -p -> async git init -> registerProjectFromPath. slug = name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g,'-') (matches project.ts:98).

GET /api/fs/list-dirs is HOME-CLAMPED: default start path = user's home (~); any resolved path that escapes home (via .. or an absolute path outside home) is rejected (400) and never listed. Mirror the static-route normalization in server.ts:234-246. Browsing outside home is a NonGoal for this minimal flow.

Route placement: POST /api/projects goes into the EXISTING src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts (Effect HttpRouter.add, merged in projectsRouteLayer). The folder browser GET /api/fs/list-dirs goes into a NEW src/dashboard/server/routes/fs.ts, imported + merged into makeRoutesLayer in server.ts.

Per-route auth (NOT global): POST /api/projects must call rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest(request) (CSRF/mutation gate, like settings.ts:811 / issues.ts); GET /api/fs/list-dirs must call rejectUnauthorizedDashboardRequest(request) (read gate, like discovered-sessions.ts). Async exec pattern = `const execAsync = promisify(exec)` as in routes/resources.ts:29.

PAN-1969 coordination: the wiring bead implements onNewProject() to open the modal AND ensures a '+ New Project' affordance exists in the sidebar PROJECTS header + Home Projects section. If PAN-1969 already added the trigger/stub, replace the stub body and do NOT add a duplicate button. Keeps PAN-1970 independently shippable/demoable even if PAN-1969 has not merged.

Frontend modal uses the CSS-module overlay/dialog pattern matching ForkModal + command-deck.module.css (.forkHelpOverlay/.forkHelpDialog/.forkHeader/.forkFooter), not inline Tailwind. Folder picker is its own subcomponent so it is independently testable. API calls use the cookie-auth fetchWithTimeout helper (src/lib/apiFetch.ts); after success invalidate React Query keys ['command-deck-projects'] and ['registered-projects'] and select the new project (setSelectedProjectKey + usePanesStore.ensureHome).

'Add existing' accepts any folder (git repo or not) — registration does not require .git; git hooks install only when .git is present (preserves projectAddCommand behavior today). No-loss refactor: `pan project add` keeps identical resulting projects.yaml entry + console next-steps output.

hazards.summary: Cross-issue coordination with PAN-1969 (onNewProject handler + '+ New Project' button). PAN-1969 may or may not be merged when this work runs (only PRDs exist on main as of 8aabbadb68).
hazards.mitigation: Wiring bead detects an existing onNewProject/stub + trigger and reuses it; only adds the button if absent. Never render two '+ New Project' triggers.

hazards.summary: Path traversal in GET /api/fs/list-dirs — a naive readdir of an attacker-supplied ?path= could list arbitrary host directories.
hazards.mitigation: Resolve + clamp to home; reject `..` escapes and absolute paths outside home (D4). Dedicated security test is a required AC (AC-4). Reuse the normalization shape from server.ts:234-246.

hazards.summary: Partial write on failure — creating the folder / git init and THEN failing registration would leave an orphan dir or a half-registered project.
hazards.mitigation: Sequence per D3: all rejections (dup key, non-empty target) happen before any mkdir/git init. AC-3 test asserts no projects.yaml mutation on the rejection paths.

hazards.summary: git init via execSync would freeze the dashboard event loop (PAN-70).
hazards.mitigation: Use execAsync (promisify(exec)) or spawn, like routes/resources.ts. AC asserts no new execSync import in server code (NFR-1).

hazards.summary: Bypassing the single write door — writing projects.yaml directly would violate NFR-2 / single-source-of-truth.
hazards.mitigation: Register ONLY via registerProjectSync (through registerProjectFromPath). Do not add any new projects.yaml writer. AC-6.

hazards.summary: Skills <-> CLI lockstep — changing pan project add flags/help would require updating skills/pan-* in the same commit (lint-skills.sh fails CI on drift).
hazards.mitigation: The refactor must keep the projectAddCommand CLI surface (flags + help strings) identical (D9). If any help text changes, update the matching skill in the same commit.

hazards.summary: preTrustDirectory has both preTrustDirectorySync (workspace-manager.ts:1100) and preTrustDirectory (1581) exports; projectAddCommand dynamically imports preTrustDirectory.
hazards.mitigation: Helper calls the SAME symbol projectAddCommand uses today (preTrustDirectory). Verify the export at implementation time; keep the existing non-fatal try/catch around it.

sessionHistory.note: Auto-planning session for PAN-1970: minimal New Project dashboard flow (add existing / new folder + git init -> register). 8 beads across shared registration core, POST /api/projects (existing+new), GET /api/fs/list-dirs, folder picker, modal, and wiring.

pipeline.verificationNotes:
Acceptance criteria check FAILED — 14/33 AC incomplete:

### Extend POST /api/projects with mode='new' (mkdir + async git init) (5/5 incomplete)
  - [ ] POST /api/projects {mode:'new', parentDir, name} creates <parentDir>/<slug(name)>, runs git init, registers key=slug(name), and returns 200 { key, name, path } with path === the created folder.
  - [ ] After a successful 'new' create, the target folder contains a .git directory (git init actually ran).
  - [ ] POST 'new' where the target exists and is non-empty returns 409 and makes no filesystem change and no registration (no partial write).
  - [ ] POST 'new' whose derived key duplicates an existing project returns 409 before any mkdir/git init runs (no orphan folder).
  - [ ] A route test covers new-mode success (folder + .git + registered), non-empty-target -> 409 (no fs/registry change), and dup-key -> 409; and asserts no new `execSync` import was added to the route.

### New Project modal (toggle + both modes + path preview + Start -> POST) (5/5 incomplete)
  - [ ] The modal renders an 'Add existing | New project' toggle; switching the toggle swaps between the folder-picker (existing) form and the name+parent (new) form.
  - [ ] In 'New project' mode the live preview displays `<parent>/<slug(name)>` and updates as the name input changes (e.g. 'My App' -> <parent>/my-app).
  - [ ] Clicking Start sends the correct POST body to /api/projects: { mode:'existing', path } in existing mode and { mode:'new', parentDir, name } in new mode.
  - [ ] When the server returns an error response, the modal displays the error message inline and stays open (no crash, no navigation).
  - [ ] A co-located Vitest test (mocked fetch) covers toggle switching, the new-mode slug preview, and the correct POST body per mode.

### Wire onNewProject() to open the modal; on success refetch lists + select project (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] A single '+ New Project' affordance in the sidebar PROJECTS header (and Home Projects section) opens the New Project modal via onNewProject(); no duplicate trigger is rendered if PAN-1969 already added one.
  - [ ] On a successful create the app invalidates/refetches both ['command-deck-projects'] and ['registered-projects'] so the new project appears in the sidebar and Home without a reload.
  - [ ] On a successful create the app opens the new project: it calls setSelectedProjectKey(key) and usePanesStore.ensureHome(key) with the new key.
  - [ ] A Vitest test asserts the success path invalidates both query keys and selects the new project key.
